summarization.a: The figures illustrate a two-step process involving the removal of SnF ligands through ligand exchange
summarization.b: The figure shows the subsequent fluorination of Al2O3 surface.

data_extraction.a:
| Description                | Reactants/Conditions       | Products/Surface State            |
|---------------------------|----------------------------------|---------------------------------------|
|Removal step: Ligand Exchange | Surface: Al₂O₃ with Al–F species; Sn precursor (O–Sn–O ligand) | Sn species bonded to surface; Al–F partially removed |
| Reaction                  | O–Sn–O ligand exchanges with Al–F groups | Formation of O–SnF species on surface |
data_extraction.b:
| Description                | Reactants/Conditions       | Products/Surface State            |
|---------------------------|----------------------------------|---------------------------------------|
| Modification Step: Fluorination | Surface: Al₂O₃ with Sn species; HF introduced | Surface becomes fluorinated (Al–F groups restored); H₂O byproduct |
| Reaction                  | HF reacts with Sn species | Sn removed; Al–F coverage increased |

a.question: What are the proposed reactions by the schematic?
a.answer:
Proposed reaction mechanism for Al2O3 ALE shows
 (a)Sn(acac)2 reaction 
(b) HF reaction

a.question: What is a widely studies ALE process?
a.answer: One of the widely studied implementations of thermal isotropic ALE at constant wafer temperature is etching of Al2O3 with alternating HF (for instance in the form of HF-pyridine) and Sn (acac)2 gases.

b.question: Does a  successful modification step work by adding reactive sites to which the reactants can easily bond to in the removal step?
How does ALE of Al2O3 work with HF and Sn(acac)2?
b.answer:
The metal oxide surface is converted into metal fluoride by HF in the modification step. The removal of aluminum atoms follows via a ligand-exchange reaction. Sn(acac)2 exchanges its acac ligands with the fluorine at the AlF3
surface. This results in the formation of volatile SnF(acac) and Al (acac)3 reaction products. The term “ligand-exchange” comes from this reaction mechanism. Modification and removal steps require two sequential or parallel surface reactions to convert Al2O3 first into AlF3 and then into Al(acac)3.
